steps.title: Use the square diagonal
steps.content: For a square inscribed in a circle, the diagonal of the square is the diameter of the circle. The diagonal connects opposite vertices on the circle and passes through the center.

steps.title: Find the diameter
steps.content:
The diameter is twice the radius:
$$2\cdot\frac{18\sqrt{2}}{2}=18\sqrt{2}$$

steps.title: Solve for the side length
steps.content: If the square's side length is $s$, its diagonal is $s\sqrt{2}$. Set that equal to the circle's diameter: $$s\sqrt{2}=18\sqrt{2}$$ $$s=18$$ The answer is A.
